1. Document Control
   Purpose: To establish the foundational metadata for the document, ensuring traceability and accountability.
   Expected Content: Document title, versioning details, approval authority, and revision history.
   Sample Elements:
   1.1 Document Title: Architecture Principles Document
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author

2. Executive Summary
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance within the organization.
   Expected Content: Summary of the architecture principles, their importance, and alignment with organizational goals.
   Sample Elements: Overview of foundational architecture principles and their role in enhancing data governance.

3. Domain and Context Framing
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the document within the organizational framework.
   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and current maturity positioning.
   Sample Elements:
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Architecture (DA)
   3.2 Organizational Context: Overview of the organization’s data management landscape.
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 0 - Non-existent governance structure.

4. Action Definition
   Purpose: To clearly articulate the action being defined, including its objectives and scope.
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objectives, and scope boundaries.
   Sample Elements:
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DA-01 - Define foundational architecture principles and scope
   4.2 Action Objective: Establish foundational architecture principles to guide data governance.
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applicable to all core data domains identified.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To outline the procedural steps necessary to achieve the defined action.
   Expected Content: Objectives, prerequisites, deliverables, and a detailed step-by-step execution structure.
   Sample Elements:
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To define architecture principles and structural scope.
   5.2 Prerequisites: Identification of core data domains.
   5.3 Deliverables: Architecture principles document.
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:
   - Identify Core Data Domains
   - Assemble Cross-Functional Team
   - Draft Initial Architecture Principles
   - Review and Validate Principles
   - Align with Governance Expectations
   - Finalize Architecture Principles Document
   - Obtain Approval from Governance Body
   - Communicate and Implement Principles

6. Governance Structural Components
   Purpose: To delineate the governance framework that supports the execution of the action.
   Expected Content: Coverage of organizational and data assets, governance themes, structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.
   Sample Elements:
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All business units involved in data management.
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: Core data domains identified in the procedure.
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, data security, data lifecycle management.
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office.
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined roles of Data Owners and Data Stewards.
   6.6 Governance Processes:
   - Establish data standards
   - Monitor compliance with governance policies
   - Facilitate data stewardship activities
   - Conduct training and awareness sessions
   - Review and update governance documents
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Interaction with IT governance and compliance frameworks.
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Areas not covered by this document, such as operational data management practices.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms
   Purpose: To define the formal control and oversight mechanisms applicable to this action.
   Expected Content: Basic governance oversight definition aligned with foundational maturity.
8. Document Governance
   Purpose: To outline the management and review processes for the document itself.
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and ownership details.
   Sample Elements:
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually.
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by the Data Governance Council.
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Governance Office.

9. Appendices
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials that support the main document.
   Expected Content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.
   Sample Elements:
   9.1 Templates: Architecture principles template.
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Previous governance documents for context.

   4.2 Action Objective: Establish foundational principles to guide architecture development.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To outline the procedural steps necessary to achieve the action objective.
   Expected Content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and step-by-step execution structure.

   - 2) Draft structural blueprints
   - 3) Align with metadata and lifecycle structures

   - 5) Incorporate quality and semantic rules
   - 6) Iterate using CMMI practices
   - 7) Finalize reference architecture
   - 8) Obtain stakeholder approval

6. Governance Structural Components
   Purpose: To delineate the structural components of the governance framework.
   Expected Content: Coverage areas, themes, organizational structure, decision authority, governance processes, interfaces, and exclusions.
   Sample Elements:
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data assets within the Data Architecture domain
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, data lifecycle management, data security
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Hierarchical representation of governance roles
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined roles and responsibilities for decision-making
   6.6 Governance Processes:
   - Establish principles
   - Draft blueprints
   - Validate components
   - Review and iterate
   - Finalize architecture
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Interaction with Data Quality and Data Security governance frameworks
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Excludes operational data management processes
